per_turn_leaks.student_message: Worsening dyspnea with a productive cough — that combination raises concern. You mentioned fever. With fever and productive cough, have you had any night sweats or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I have been having some pretty intense night sweats, to be honest. It's like my whole body is just drenched in sweat when I wake up, even if it's cold outside.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Night sweats with fever and productive cough — that's a classic infectious triad. With respiratory infections, pleuritic involvement is common. Have you had any chest pain, especially when breathing deeply or coughing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've been having some sharp pains on my right side of my chest, which gets worse when I breathe in deeply or cough. It's a bit hard to describe, but it feels like someone is stabbing me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Right-sided pleuritic pain fits with a lower lobe process. You mentioned allergic rhinitis — allergies and asthma often co-occur. Have you ever had wheezing episodes before this illness? Any history of asthma?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've had wheezing episodes since I was a kid, around age 8 or so. They would happen whenever I got a cold or exercised too much, but they never really seemed to be a big deal and I never really talked to a doctor about them. It's always just been kind of a background thing for me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Recurrent wheezing since childhood that was never diagnosed — that's very likely undiagnosed asthma. Your mother might have a history too. Does anyone in your family have asthma or other lung conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom actually has asthma, so I'm pretty sure she would be able to relate to what I've been going through. And, yeah, that makes sense about my childhood wheezing episodes... maybe it was all connected and just wasn't properly diagnosed back then?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your mother having asthma strengthens the genetic link. Speaking of family history — any other respiratory or serious conditions in the family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my grandmother passed away from lung cancer when I was younger... it's been a bit on my mind lately, but I'm not sure if that's related to what I'm going through now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: With this level of illness — fever, productive cough, night sweats, chest pain — your body is fighting hard. How has your appetite been? Any weight changes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my appetite hasn't been great lately. I've just lost interest in food and haven't been eating much at all for the past few days. And yeah, I'm sure I've lost some weight because of it... I just feel so exhausted all the time that even thinking about cooking a meal seems like too much effort.
